codeReviewIssues.description: frame_period_ns(0) returns 16_666_667 (hardcoded fallback) while frame_period_ns(60) returns 16_666_666 (integer division 1e9/60). The 1ns difference is functionally irrelevant but is an inconsistency — the 0fps fallback is nominally 60fps but differs by 1ns from the computed 60fps period. Tests correctly capture both values.

The main loop performs three wait syscalls per iteration: one blocking wait on both handles, then two non-blocking polls to determine which handle(s) signaled. This works correctly because wait is level-triggered, but adds 2 extra syscalls per loop iteration. Acceptable for prototype; a production system might use the return value of the first wait to determine which handle was ready.

scheduler.on_render_complete() is called at three dispersed locations in the render loop: (1) empty change list path, (2) no damage rects path, (3) after GPU present. Each is correct for its code path, but the dispersed call sites increase risk of missed calls if new early-exit paths are added. Consider extracting a render_and_present() helper or using a drop guard pattern to ensure on_render_complete is always called when the render phase is entered.

sharedStateObservations.description: Worker's skillFeedback suggests the os-worker skill should mention that stale QEMU instances from other workers may be running and consuming CPU, causing boot timeouts. Recommend adding guidance to kill stale instances by their unique disk image paths before launching visual tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The os-worker skill should mention that multiple QEMU instances from other workers may be running and consuming CPU, causing boot timeouts.'

sharedStateObservations.description: The worker correctly updated .factory/library/architecture.md with the frame scheduler documentation. No gap here — noting as positive example of shared state maintenance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit 3bfae60 adds 12 lines to architecture.md documenting the frame scheduler pattern (timer-driven, two-handle wait, event coalescing, idle optimization, initial frame outside loop).

sharedStateObservations.description: AGENTS.md test count says ~1555 but actual count at this commit is 1593 (20 new frame_scheduler tests + 1573 existing). The test count should be updated to reflect the growing suite.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md line: 'cargo test -- --test-threads=1 in system/test/ must pass (all ~1555 tests)'. Handoff shows 1593 tests passing.

summary: Clean pass. The frame scheduler is a well-designed pure state machine with excellent separation of concerns — no syscall dependencies, fully testable on host. The compositor integration correctly implements two-handle wait (core + timer), event coalescing via dirty flag, and idle optimization. 20 tests comprehensively cover all 8 assigned VAL assertions (001, 002, 003, 005, 006, 011, 012, 013). No unsafe code introduced. No kernel changes required. The one-shot timer recreation pattern matches the established convention from core's clock timer. Three non-blocking observations noted: a 1ns inconsistency in the 0fps fallback period, extra poll syscalls in the main loop (correct but could be optimized), and dispersed on_render_complete() call sites. All 33 frame_scheduler tests pass (including 13 added by the subsequent frame-budgeting commit). Build succeeds. QEMU visual verification confirmed display pipeline renders correctly with the frame scheduler active.
